dialogueChunks.intro: The grief has turned to anger, {player_name}. And angry people make dangerous decisions.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A group has decided to avenge the dead by striking the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint}. They're not soldiers: they're workers blinded by rage. {target_npc} tried to stop them but was shouted down. If they march on that camp, they'll be slaughtered. I need you to talk to {target_npc} and help convince the group to stand down.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we can't channel this grief into something constructive, {quest_stakes_the} will suffer even more loss.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the loss keeps compounding. Another family is leaving.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: More families packed up and left in the night. They said they can't raise children in a place where death comes so easily. {target_npc} is trying to hold things together, but every departure takes skills and hope with it. The departing families left behind {quest_item} in their haste {location_hint}. Retrieve what they abandoned before it is lost to the elements.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're bleeding people as surely as if the {enemy_type} were still inside our walls. {quest_stakes_The} depends on having enough hands to work and stand watch.

dialogueChunks.intro: Something unexpected happened, {player_name}. The grief brought something else to the surface.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: While searching near {quest_focus_the}, {target_npc} found evidence that the raid wasn't random. The {enemy_type} targeted specific buildings, specific stores, as if guided by someone who knew our layout. There might be a collaborator: someone who traded information about our defenses for personal safety.
dialogueChunks.outro: If someone sold out their neighbors to save themselves, the dead deserve justice and the living deserve the truth. Retrieve {quest_item} from the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint} as proof.
